data.title: Weekly Recap: Kospi Ends Slightly Lower Amid Tech Weakness
data.description:
South Korea’s benchmark Kospi index closed the week marginally lower, as weakness in technology heavyweights and cautious foreign flows offset midweek gains, keeping the index near the 2,650 level.

The Kospi began the week on a soft note on April 13, slipping below 2,630 points amid muted regional sentiment and early profit-taking. The market staged a modest rebound over the next two sessions, briefly climbing toward the 2,660 range on selective buying.

Despite the midweek recovery, trading remained largely range-bound, with the index fluctuating between approximately 2,620 and 2,670 points. Gains proved difficult to sustain as investors remained wary of external uncertainties.

By the end of trading week, the Kospi edged lower, trimming earlier advances to finish the week slightly in negative territory.

Technology stocks, particularly semiconductor plays, were a key drag on the market, reflecting cautious sentiment around global demand and valuation concerns. This offset support from financials and selected industrial counters.

Foreign investors were broadly cautious during the week, contributing to intermittent volatility and limiting upside momentum.

Investor sentiment remained closely tied to global developments, including US interest rate expectations and broader risk appetite. The lack of strong catalysts kept market direction subdued.

The near-term outlook for the Kospi remains cautious, with the 2,650-level acting as a key pivot. Sustained upside will depend on a rebound in technology stocks and clearer global signals, while downside risks persist amid external volatility and shifting fund flows.

data.title: Weekly Snapshot: STI Ends Flat As Late Gains Offset Early Weakness
data.description:
The benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) closed little changed for the week, as late buying in banking heavyweights helped the market recover from early declines, leaving the index hovering near the 3,200 level.

The STI began the week on a softer footing on April 13, pressured by cautious regional sentiment and mild profit-taking. The index dipped below 3,180 points in early trading before stabilising midweek, as investors adopted a wait-and-see stance amid mixed global cues.

Trading remained largely range-bound throughout the week, with the STI moving within a narrow band of roughly 3,170 to 3,220 points. Market conviction was limited, as participants balanced external uncertainties with selective bargain hunting.

By April 17, the index edged higher on mild bargain buying, trimming earlier losses and ending the week broadly flat.

Gains were primarily supported by Singapore’s key banking stocks, including DBS Group, Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation and United Overseas Bank, which lent stability to the index. Their defensive appeal and steady earnings outlook helped underpin sentiment.

However, upside momentum was capped by weakness in selected property and industrial counters, alongside intermittent profit-taking in recent outperformers.

Investor sentiment continued to track broader regional and global market trends, including interest rate expectations and economic data releases. The lack of strong directional catalysts kept trading subdued.

Looking ahead, the STI is expected to remain range-bound in the near term, with the 3,200-level acting as a key psychological marker. Sustained upside may depend on stronger foreign inflows and clearer global macro signals, while downside risks remain tied to external volatility.

data.title: Market Review: Hang Seng Ends Marginally Lower Amid Choppy Trade
data.description:
The Hang Seng Index (HSI) closed the week slightly in the red, as volatile trading and late-session selling offset midweek optimism, with the benchmark hovering near the 26,000-mark.

The HSI started the week on a cautious footing, slipping below 25,700 points on April 13 amid subdued sentiment. Bargain hunting emerged in subsequent sessions, lifting the index gradually toward the 25,900-26,000 range by midweek.

Trading remained uneven throughout the week, with the index oscillating within a band of roughly 25,600 to 26,200 points. While intermittent buying in technology and select blue chips provided support, gains were frequently pared by profit-taking.

By April 17, the index edged lower, erasing part of its earlier recovery to finish the week marginally down.

Technology counters and new-economy stocks lent some resilience, supported by selective investor interest. However, weakness in financials and property stocks weighed on overall performance, reflecting lingering macro concerns.

Market sentiment was also tempered by external uncertainties, including global rate outlooks and regional economic signals, which kept investors on the sidelines.

Despite secondary market softness, Hong Kong’s IPO pipeline remained active, with strong debut performances underscoring continued liquidity in the market and investor appetite for growth names.

The near-term outlook for the HSI remains cautious, with the 26,000-level serving as a key psychological threshold. Sustained upside will likely depend on improved global risk appetite and stronger institutional inflows, while downside risks persist amid external volatility.

data.title: HSIF Fails To Break Higher, RHB Says Stay Short
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has recommended maintaining short positions on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), citing a negative trading bias as the index struggles below the 26,600-point resistance level.

The research house said HSIF retreated 169 points to close at 26,182 points last Friday after failing to extend its rebound, having opened at 26,356 points before slipping to an intraday low of 25,030 points. Despite a later rebound in evening trade to around 26,502 points, analysts viewed the pullback as a sign of temporary price weakness.

RHB Research noted that the index’s inability to sustain gains above 26,600 points suggests rangebound movement in the near term, with momentum indicators offering little conviction. The relative strength index is hovering near the 50% level, indicating a lack of strong directional momentum.

As a result, the research house said it is maintaining its negative stance, advising traders to keep short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage downside risk.

On key levels, support is seen at 25,100 points, followed by 24,500 points, while resistance remains at 26,600 points and 27,200 points.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Ends Higher As Defensive Buying Lifts Blue Chips
data.description:
The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) closed higher on April 20, supported by buying interest in large-cap defensive counters as investors navigated rising geopolitical uncertainty.

The benchmark index rose 7.09 points, or 0.42%, to 1,702.30 at 5pm, recovering from an intraday low of 1,689.00 and nearing its session high of 1,703.64.

Gains in the broader market, meanwhile, were mixed, with the FBM Emas up 25.18 points (0.2) to 12,543.35 and the FBM KLCI supported by selective accumulation in heavyweight stocks, while the FBM 70 Index and FBM Sha Index slipped, indicating a tilt toward safer, large-cap names.

Market activity remained robust, with several actively traded counters driving volumes. Zetrix AI Bhd led turnover, rising two sen to 85.5 sen, followed by Inari Amertron Bhd, which gained 12 sen to RM1.77.

AirAsia X Bhd and Empire Premium Food Bhd were among the most actively traded laggards, both ending lower, while Sunway Healthcare Holdings Bhd posted gains amid continued interest in selected healthcare-related counters.

The market’s upward bias reflects a cautious rotation into defensive plays, as investors balance risk exposure against ongoing global uncertainties, with large-cap resilience helping to anchor the index in positive territory at the close.

data.title: Stock Picks: Mr DIY And TIME dotCom
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Mr DIY Group Bhd and TIME dotCom Bhd as near-term trading opportunities, citing strengthening technical momentum and potential upside from key breakout levels.

The research house said Mr DIY has entered a bullish phase after breaking above the RM1.60 resistance on strong trading volume, signalling growing buying interest. It noted that the stock’s upward move has been supported by increasing participation, indicating that momentum could sustain in the near term.

RHB Research expects the counter to test the next resistance levels at RM1.70 and RM1.80 if the positive trend continues. However, it cautioned that a drop below the RM1.50 support level could trigger a correction.

Meanwhile, TIME dotCom is approaching a potential breakout after testing its immediate resistance with a bullish candlestick pattern. The stock has been consolidating below the RM6.05 level, with its 21-day simple moving average trending upwards, suggesting improving momentum.

The research house said a successful break above resistance could push the stock towards RM6.30, with further upside to RM6.55. On the downside, a breach below RM5.80 may signal the start of a correction phase.

RHB Research said both counters are showing signs of strengthening technical setups, with price action and volume trends pointing to increasing bullish traction in the near term.

data.title: KLCI Slips As Middle East Tensions Flare, Oil Rebound Dents Risk Appetite
data.description:
The optimism that lifted Wall Street on Friday failed to carry through to Bursa Malaysia, as renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East weighed on sentiment at the start of the week.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI slipped 1.71 points to 1,693.50, extending its sideways trend as investors stayed cautious amid escalating developments in the oil market and global trade routes.

According to The Star, market sentiment turned risk-off after reports of renewed disruption to the Strait of Hormuz, following accusations of ceasefire breaches and retaliatory moves involving Iran and US-linked shipping restrictions.

The development triggered a rebound in crude oil prices, with Brent crude futures recovering from below US$90 a barrel on Friday to around US$95 a barrel on Monday.

Apex Securities said the latest escalation has reversed last week’s risk-on sentiment, with higher oil prices likely to weigh on broader market confidence despite potential support for energy-linked counters.

The research house noted that while energy stocks may benefit from firmer crude prices, sectors more sensitive to input costs such as consumer and transport-related companies could come under renewed pressure. It also highlighted the likelihood of investors rotating into defensive plays and locking in gains from recent outperformers.

Despite the cautious tone, select counters remained active. Pharmaniaga was flat at 24 sen while AirAsia X slipped 1 sen to RM1.28 and Aizo edged up 0.5 sen to 4 sen.

Regionally, sentiment was mixed, with Japan’s Nikkei gaining 0.77% to 58,913 and South Korea’s Kospi rising 0.97% to 6,251, supported by continued momentum in technology shares.

data.title: Singapore Market Opens Softer With Index Below 5,000 Mark
data.description:
Singapore equities opened slightly lower, with the Straits Times Index (STI) slipping 13.10 points, or 0.26%, to 4,984.83 as at 9.10am, tracking a cautious start across regional markets.

Market breadth was negative, with decliners outpacing advancers 128 to 104, while total trading volume stood at 169.80 million shares valued at S$216.72 million.

Among index heavyweights, DBS Group Holdings traded at S$56.98, while OCBC Bank stood at S$22.59 and Singtel at S$4.75. Other actively watched counters included Yangzijiang Shipbuilding at S$4.10, City Developments at S$8.77 and Keppel Corporation at S$11.78.

Across broader indices, the iEdge S-REIT Index was at 1,078.41, while the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index stood at 1,541.50, reflecting a mixed tone beyond the benchmark.

In the derivatives market, activity was modest with total volume at 16,670 contracts. FTSE China A50 Index Futures for April traded at 15,412.00, while Nikkei 225 Index Futures for June stood at 59,065.00.

The softer opening comes as investors remain cautious amid broader regional uncertainties, with early trading indicating a wait-and-see stance across sectors.

data.title: Decliners Dominate Bursa As Losers Outpace Gainers At Close
data.description:
Decliners led the market at Bursa Malaysia’s close, with selling pressure outweighing gains as cautious sentiment persisted across the broader market.

At 5pm, losers outnumbered gainers 657 to 499, while 573 counters ended unchanged, reflecting a negative market breadth despite pockets of strength in selected heavyweights.

Among the top decliners, Nestlé (M) Bhd fell RM1 to RM98, followed by United Plantations Bhd, which dropped 58 sen to RM33.60. Petronas Dagangan Bhd slid 38 sen to RM20.20, while Allianz Malaysia Bhd and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd also ended lower at RM20.90 and RM5.31.

On the upside, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd led gainers, rising 46 sen to RM31.46, while Malayan Banking Bhd advanced 24 sen to RM11.34, lending some support to the benchmark index.

Other notable gainers included Heineken Malaysia Bhd and Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, each posting moderate gains.

Despite selective buying interest, the broader market tone remained cautious, with investors continuing to navigate external uncertainties and sector-specific pressures heading into the close.

data.title: Stock Today: Empire Premium Gained 0.96% Amid Second Day Trading
data.description:
Empire Premium Food Bhd extended its post listing momentum into the second trading day, with analysts noting continued strong investor appetite following its Main Market debut surge.

The stock remains a BUY–rated counter according to earlier coverage from PublicInvest, with a fair value of 98 sen, reflecting optimism on its scalable outlet expansion model and high traffic retail positioning.

On its latest trading session, Empire Premium last changed hands at RM1.05, up 0.96% with intraday movement between RM1.01 and RM1.07. Volume remained active at 220.4 million shares, although noticeably lower than debut day levels.

The counter opened at RM1.05 before consolidating within a tight range, suggesting early-stage price discovery after the IPO driven rally.

The strong start follows a volatile but highly enthusiastic listing on 17 April 2026, when the stock surged as much as 57% in early trade after opening at RM1.10 versus its IPO price of RM0.70. It later settled at RM1.04 on debut, still marking a 48.6% gain, with trading volumes exceeding 266 million shares as retail and institutional demand surged.

Investor sentiment has been buoyed by Empire Premium’s underlying fundamentals, with the group reporting a profit after tax of RM35.1 million on revenue of RM216.8 million for the nine months ended Dec 31, 2025, driven mainly by its grab and go outlet network.

Analysts have pointed to its expansion potential in malls and transit hubs as a key growth driver, although near term volatility is expected as early investors lock in gains.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds Near US$75,000 As Ethereum Stabilises In Volatile Trade
data.description:
Bitcoin hovered near the US$75,000 mark on April 20, consolidating recent gains after a volatile week, while Ethereum held steady above US$2,300 as investors weighed macro and geopolitical signals.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency traded in a tight range after briefly pushing toward the US$76,000 level in recent sessions, supported by improving risk sentiment and easing geopolitical tensions.

Ethereum, meanwhile, showed resilience, maintaining levels above US$2,300 despite broader market swings, signalling continued investor confidence in the second-largest digital asset.

Crypto markets have been oscillating between bullish inflows and macro headwinds, with recent rallies driven by renewed capital entering the sector and optimism over institutional adoption.

However, analysts caution that momentum remains fragile, with price action still highly sensitive to global developments and interest rate expectations, even as technical indicators point to underlying strength.

The latest moves underscore a market in consolidation mode, with Bitcoin testing breakout levels and Ethereum holding firm, as traders look for the next catalyst to drive the crypto complex higher.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Edges Higher At Midday Despite Lingering Caution Over Hormuz Tensions
data.description:
FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) extended gains at midday, tracking firmer regional markets, though investor sentiment remained cautious amid ongoing developments in the Strait of Hormuz.

At 12:30pm, the benchmark index rose 5.11 points, or 0.3%, to 1,700.32 from its close of 1,695.21 on April 17, after moving between an intraday low of 1,689.00 and a high of 1,703.64.

Broader market performance was mixed, with the FBM Emas Index up 22.31 points (0.18%) to 12,540.48 and the FBM 70 Index was marginally lower at 17,722.66 after easing 1.07 points (0.01%), reflecting uneven sentiment across segments.

Market breadth stayed negative, with losers outpacing gainers 604 to 418, while 530 counters were unchanged, underscoring the cautious tone despite the headline index gains.

Among heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd rose 12 sen to RM11.22, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd gained 13 sen to RM7.60 and Public Bank Bhd edged up to RM4.69. IHH Healthcare Bhd added two sen to RM8.75, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd slipped two sen to RM14.30.

On the leaderboard, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd topped gainers, rising 42 sen to RM31.42, followed by Hong Leong Industries Bhd and Hong Leong Bank Bhd. Meanwhile, Nestlé (M) Bhd led decliners, dropping RM1.38 to RM97.62, alongside losses in Petronas Dagangan Bhd and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd.

Trading activity remained brisk, with turnover at 1.75 billion units valued at RM1.33 billion.

Among the most active counters, Zetrix AI Bhd dominated volumes, followed by AirAsia X Bhd and Inari Amertron Bhd.

Despite the modest uptick in the benchmark index, the broader market’s negative breadth signals lingering investor caution as geopolitical risks continue to cloud near-term outlook.

data.title: Oil Jumps, Stocks Wobble As Mideast Ceasefire Turns Shaky
data.description:
Oil prices jumped and stock futures fell on Monday (Apr 20) as rising tension in the Middle East kept shipping in and out of the Gulf to a minimum, though traders held out hope for a resolution and Asia’s stock markets breezed toward record highs.

Brent crude futures rose about 5 per cent to US$95.16 a barrel. S&P 500 futures fell around 0.6 per cent and European futures fell 1.1 per cent. But equity benchmarks in Seoul, Taipei and Tokyo shrugged off risks to advance, with Taiwan’s shares touching a record high and the other two not far behind.Iran has re-imposed its de facto closure of the Strait of Hormuz, though Kpler data showed that more than 20 vessels carrying oil products, metals, gas and fertiliser passed through it on Saturday, the busiest day for the chokepoint since Mar 1.

The ceasefire in the Iran war, due to run until Tuesday, was in doubt after the US seized an Iranian cargo ship and Tehran’s top military command vowed to retaliate.

“The headlines look bad; it looks like there’s disagreement … which has led to a little bit of re-escalation,” said Damien Boey, portfolio strategist at Wilson Asset Management in Sydney.

“But I think, ultimately, both sides want to be able to do a deal – that’s part of the reason why the market’s optimistic and not selling off too much.”

Hong Kong’s Hang Seng rose 0.8 per cent, Japan’s Nikkei climbed 1 per cent and South Korea’s KOSPI rose 1.4 per cent.

One of the strongest notes of caution in markets on Monday came from Australia’s largest business lender, National Australia Bank, which flagged a US$500 million impairment charge as it expects the war to drive up bad debts.Bonds, which rallied on Friday, retreated and the yield on benchmark 10-year Treasuries rose 2.2 basis points to 4.266 per cent.

The dollar – which was sold for the best part of the past two weeks – steadied, buying 158.8 yen and trading at US$1.1760 per euro.

Wall Street indexes touched record highs on Friday, supported by expectations of robust first-quarter earnings, the bulk of which come this week.

British inflation data, US retail sales and European purchasing managers’ index figures are also due through the week, though much of markets’ focus will be on Gulf shipping.

“The critical barometer of geopolitical risk has been distilled into one data point: The number of ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz,” said Bob Savage, head of markets macro strategy at BNY.

“Peace talks matter, but the immediate focus is on oil and other supply shortages driving inflation.”

Reuters

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 20, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 20, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Stock Today: Petronas Gas Up 0.33% Amid Oil Price Volatility Concerns
data.description:
Petronas Gas Bhd edged higher by 0.33% to RM18.08 as of the time of writing, tracking cautious investor positioning amid ongoing volatility in global oil markets linked to geopolitical tensions and shifting energy supply expectations.

The utilities counter opened at RM18.02 and traded within a tight range between RM17.98 and RM18.22, before last done at RM18.08. Volume remained relatively light at 167,900 shares, suggesting measured participation despite broader energy market uncertainty.

Market observers said sentiment around energy-linked counters has been mixed as oil price movements continue to be influenced by geopolitical developments in the Middle East, alongside expectations of stable domestic energy demand.

Petronas Gas, which operates midstream gas processing and transportation infrastructure, is often viewed as a defensive proxy within the utilities space due to its regulated income structure.

The stock has remained broadly range-bound in recent sessions, with investors weighing stable earnings visibility against external macro pressures affecting the wider energy value chain.

Despite short-term volatility in sentiment, utilities counters like Petronas Gas are typically seen as less exposed to commodity swings compared with upstream oil and gas players, given their fee-based business model and long-term contract structures.

As of writing, trading activity remained subdued, reflecting a wait-and-see stance among investors as markets digest developments in global energy prices and their potential downstream impact on regional utilities earnings.

data.title: Stock Today: Ta Ann Holds Firm As Investors Weigh Cost Pressures Against Dividend Potential
data.description:
Ta Ann Holdings Bhd was little changed in late trading as investors weighed expectations of a muted first-quarter performance against improving earnings prospects ahead, with the stock last done at RM5.32, down 4 sen or 0.75% as at the time of writing.

The counter opened at RM5.36 and moved within a tight range between RM5.32 and RM5.36, reflecting cautious sentiment amid mixed sector signals for plantation and timber earnings.

Public Investment Bank Bhd said Ta Ann is expected to post a softer first-quarter performance, although earnings momentum is likely to improve in subsequent quarters. It noted that fresh fruit bunch output rose 7% year-on-year to 145,898 tonnes, providing some support to the plantation division.

However, the research house highlighted continued weakness in the timber segment, which is expected to remain loss-making due to softer selling prices and a weaker US dollar environment.

Cost pressures are also weighing on sentiment, with fertiliser prices up about 25% since the last procurement cycle in December 2025. PublicInvest said Ta Ann has locked in around 55% of its fertiliser needs for the financial year ending December 2026, which should provide some short-term cost visibility.

Transportation expenses are also expected to rise sharply following higher diesel prices, with overall production costs projected to increase by 5% to 10% year-on-year to between RM2,100 and RM2,200 per tonne.

Despite near-term pressure, PublicInvest maintained an Outperform call on Ta Ann with a target price of RM6.66, citing its net cash position of nearly RM400 million and annual free cash flow of about RM220 million, which could support sustained shareholder returns.

The firm also pointed to a favourable crude palm oil price outlook as a potential driver for higher-than-expected dividends.

data.title: Ringgit Almost Flat Against Dollar, Investors Seek Safe-Haven Assets
data.description:
The ringgit opened almost flat against the US dollar on Monday as investors sought shelter in safe-haven assets, with the greenback seen as one of the ways to preserve capital, Bernama reported.

At 8 am, the local currency eased to 3.9520/9600 against the greenback from 3.9505/9545 at last Friday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent crude prices climbed 7.29 per cent and 6.07 per cent to US$89.96 per barrel and US$95.87 per barrel, respectively, as the situation in West Asia remains highly fluid.

“The Strait of Hormuz was closed again shortly after the Iranian government announced the reopening on Saturday,” he told Bernama. 

He said the US dollar index (DXY) was trending upward, rising 0.19 per cent to 98.282 points this morning.

“On that note, the ringgit is likely to stay weak today. Time is also running short, as the two-week ceasefire would end this week.

“The immediate concern now is whether the ceasefire can be extended further to allow more diplomatic discourse to take place. For now, it’s going to be the risk-off mode,” he added. 

At the opening, the ringgit traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

It strengthened against the British pound to 5.3289/3397 from 5.3454/3508 at last Friday’s close, rose versus the euro to 4.6412/6506 from 4.6588/6635, and gained against the Japanese yen to 2.4832/4884 from 2.4838/4865.

The local currency traded mixed against its ASEAN peers.

It climbed against the Singapore dollar to 3.1033/1100 from last Friday’s close of 3.1053/1086, depreciated versus the Thai baht to 12.3096/3418 from 12.3084/3274, edged down against the Indonesian rupiah at 229.9/230.4 from 229.8/230.1 and was flat against the Philippines peso to 6.58/6.60 from 6.58/6.59.

data.title: US Stocks Fall On US-Iran Tension Flaring Up Again
data.description:
US stocks fell on Monday after a chaotic weekend of events reignited US-Iran tensions, putting peace talks at risk and leaving investors bracing for more uncertainty.

The S&P 500 shed 0.1%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite lost 0.2%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones Industrial Average wavered just below the flat line.The rapid escalation in tensions comes after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship, blowing a hole in its engine room, according to President Trump. For its part, Iran fired at vessels and abruptly stopped traffic in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, abandoning its promise to allow some to pass, as it said the US had broken a ceasefire deal.Renewed concerns about oil supply disruptions in the critical waterway and revived worries about inflation suggested prospects for talks to end the war were clouded. The Hormuz standoff is just one of the several sticking points in US-Iran negotiations.

Oil prices jumped on Monday, but remained well below the key $100 level. US benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude futures were last up 4.8% at around $86.50 per barrel after stronger losses earlier in the session, while global benchmark Brent crude popped 4.2% to near $94.

Looking ahead for the week, investors will parse another busy stretch of earnings, with major names including Tesla, Intel and United Airlines set to report in the days ahead, offering the next test for markets navigating record stock rises and increasing geopolitical risk.

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Crypto Wrap: Bitcoin Leads Gains, Ethereum Steady As Risk Appetite Returns
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market ended the week on a firmer footing, with Bitcoin leading gains and Ethereum holding steady, as improving risk sentiment and easing geopolitical tensions supported prices.

Bitcoin began the week on a cautious note, trading around the mid-US$74,000 range, reflecting broader market uncertainty. Momentum gradually improved through the week, with the world’s largest cryptocurrency climbing toward US$76,000-US$77,000 by April 17, marking its highest levels in over a month.

Ethereum, on the other hand, mirrored the trend but with more subdued gains, hovering above US$2,300 for most of the week. By April 17, the second-largest cryptocurrency remained relatively stable near US$2,300-US$2,330, indicating resilience despite market volatility.

Sentiment strengthened toward the end of the week, with both Bitcoin and Ethereum advancing alongside broader risk assets. Bitcoin rose as much as 2%-4% on April 17, supported by easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and renewed investor inflows into digital assets.

The rebound pushed Bitcoin to test the upper end of its recent trading range, suggesting a potential breakout after weeks of consolidation.

Despite the late rally, trading remained relatively range-bound earlier in the week. Bitcoin fluctuated between roughly US$74,000 and US$77,000, while Ethereum traded within a narrower band of US$2,250 to US$2,350, reflecting cautious positioning among investors.

Analysts noted that underlying technical indicators for both assets remain constructive, even as short-term volatility persists.

Market direction was largely influenced by macro developments, particularly geopolitical headlines and shifting expectations around global liquidity. Improved risk appetite and optimism over easing tensions helped drive the late-week recovery.

At the same time, the crypto market continued to track movements in equities, highlighting its sensitivity to broader financial conditions.

Looking ahead, Bitcoin appears poised to challenge resistance near the US$78,000 level, while Ethereum is expected to consolidate above US$2,300. Near-term direction will likely depend on sustained inflows and macro stability, with volatility expected to remain elevated.

data.title: Asian Stocks Rebound As Investors Look Past Middle East Tensions, Refocus On AI Rally
data.description:
Asian equities climbed on Monday as investors brushed aside renewed tensions in the Middle East, shifting focus back to corporate fundamentals and the ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) rally.

Bloomberg reported, The MSCI Asia Pacific Index rose as much as 0.8%, while the MSCI Emerging Markets Index recovered losses recorded during the nearly two-month conflict involving Iran. US futures also pared earlier declines, while oil prices and the US dollar retreated from intraday highs.

Market sentiment suggests investors are increasingly positioning for “peak uncertainty” to have passed, supported by expectations of renewed diplomatic talks and resilient earnings from key technology players. Analysts say the revival of AI-driven optimism is helping offset lingering geopolitical risks.

“I think markets are priced for an eventual deal to get on with life again in the Middle East,” said Matthew Haupt, Portfolio Manager at Wilson Asset Management.

“We’re seeing a very mature response in equity futures so far, as we all know the destination, so are largely willing to look through it now.”

Still, conviction remains low, with trading volumes across major Asian markets subdued. Investors remain cautious amid mixed signals from Washington and Tehran, particularly as statements from Donald Trump and Iranian officials cast uncertainty over the timeline for potential negotiations.

Regional benchmarks such as Japan’s Nikkei 225 and Taiwan’s Taiex index remain near record highs, supported largely by gains in semiconductor stocks. The easing of short-selling activity in Japan further indicates a reluctance among investors to adopt a bearish stance.

Technology stocks led gains, particularly in South Korea, where equities rose more than 1% on the back of strong chipmaker performance.

Earnings momentum remains robust, with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co lifting its revenue outlook on sustained AI demand, while Samsung Electronics recently posted a sharp jump in quarterly profit.

Upcoming results from SK Hynix Inc are expected to provide further direction for the sector, as investors continue to bet on the long-term strength of AI-driven growth.
